channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: India debuts hydrogen-powered train as part of sustainability drive
item.description: India joins a handful of countries that have successfully deployed the zero-emission technology in their rail networks.

item.title: World Cup third-place playoff: What are France and England playing for?
item.description: Didier Deschamps takes charge ⁠of Les Bleus for the final time while Kylian Mbappe is chasing individual history.

item.title: Knesset dissolves: How will Israel vote in October’s general election?
item.description: Knesset dissolves after first full term since 1988, with Israel heading towards crucial elections on October 27, 2026.

item.title: Has Ukraine’s Zelenskyy created a rival by sacking his defence minister?
item.description: Rallies in support of sacked Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov have exposed a political challenge for Zelenskyy.

item.title: As Chinese economy slows, experts say there will be ‘problems for Beijing’
item.description: While the 'export engine is running very hot', jobs are lagging, squeezing spending in China.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: 10 things to know before Spain-Argentina final in New York
item.description: From the viral photo of Lionel Messi with baby Lamine Yamal to Donald Trump's presence and the NFL-style half-time show.

item.title: Israel headed for October election as parliament dissolved
item.description: PM Netanyahu’s political survival at stake as Knesset dissolves before the vote.

item.title: Germany repeats call for EU force to replace UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: Berlin warns against a 'security vacuum' in Lebanon's south once UNIFIL mandate expires at year's end.

item.title: US strikes destroy key bridges, tower in southern Iran
item.description: US strikes destroy key bridges, tower in southern Iran

item.title: Watch FIFA World Cup: Argentina arrives in New Jersey ahead of Spain final
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: Gulf states come under Iranian fire as US strikes intensify
item.description: Tehran targets multiple countries across the region as the wave of US strikes on Iran steps up.

item.title: US attacks southern Iran, as Tehran hits Gulf countries: What’s the latest?
item.description: Iran struck Bahrain, Qatar, Oman, Jordan and Syria in retaliation for US strikes.

item.title: Why is Pakistan’s Sindh province facing a major child HIV outbreak?
item.description: The latest outbreak linked to a Karachi hospital is the newest in a series that experts say reflects systemic failures.

item.title: John Esposito transformed how the West understood Islam
item.description: The pioneering scholar, who died on July 15, challenged Orientalist orthodoxies and reshaped the study of Islam.

item.title: China’s Xi says AI ‘should not be a solo performance by a single country’
item.description: The Chinese leader called for more international cooperation in developing the technology at a conference in Shanghai.

item.title: Texas floods ravage through southwestern areas of state
item.description: At least 2 people are dead, and over 200 were rescued in catastrophic floods in the Texas Hill Country region.

item.title: Key takeaways from Donald Trump’s controversial speech on election security
item.description: The president claimed China and 'deep state' actors had deceived US voters, but critics say his claims were misleading.

item.title: US bombards Iran in sixth-straight night of attacks
item.description: The US has made good on threats to bomb civilian infrastructure in Iran in its sixth-straight night of attacks.

item.title: Israeli settlers attack Sinjil town in occupied West Bank
item.description: Clashes erupted after Israeli settlers attacked the outskirts of the occupied West Bank town of Sinjil.

item.title: China rebukes UK over nationalisation of British Steel
item.description: The UK has appropriated its last working steelworks following fears its former Chinese owners would shut it down.

item.title: Which players are favourites to be FIFA World Cup 2026 award winners?
item.description: Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe are among the biggest stars expected to walk away with at least one individual award.

item.title: Iranian sources say US strikes hit civilian infrastructure
item.description: Iranian social media accounts, and local sources allege that US strikes caused casualties and hit infrastructure.

item.title: IEA chief warns Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens global energy security
item.description: Fatih Birol says 'oil security is still a critical issue' and world should be 'worried' if situation does not improve.

item.title: Goa’s forgotten orchata drink lives on in family kitchens
item.description: Once reserved for elite Catholic households, this almond drink survives thanks to Goans preserving tradition.

item.title: How three Ivorian firms are competing with global brands
item.description: Homegrown firms are gaining ground in Ivory Coast's fuel, finance and cosmetics sectors.
